cross_scene_arcs.title: The Weight of the Wager
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Dr. Parnassus confronts the escalating consequences of his ancient supernatural pact with Mr. Nick, evolving from haunted memory to direct confrontation and finally to a desperate new wager to save his daughter.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Parnassus relives the origin of his fated bargain with Mr. Nick, burdened by dread and inevitability.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He commits to a new 'first to five' soul race to save Valentina, reigniting the cosmic conflict with renewed urgency.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The extreme difficulty of winning the bet in time, given his deteriorating health and the troupe's instability, remains a looming threat.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Fractured Fatherhood
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Parnassus's strained relationship with Valentina deteriorates under the weight of paternal guilt and control, collapses after her capture, and culminates in a bittersweet reunion that exposes the emotional cost of his mission.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Tensions flare between Parnassus and Valentina due to unresolved grief and his authoritarian control over the mission.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: They are reunited after decades, but emotional distance and divergent lives prevent true reconciliation.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The mutual disappointment and years of absence remain unhealed, leaving their future relationship uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Collapse of Authority
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Parnassus's leadership erodes as his physical and emotional decline leads to public humiliation, loss of control, and eventual abdication of his role, forcing others to act in his stead.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: His authority is first defied when Valentina and Anton revive the mysterious man against his orders.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He becomes emotionally shattered and passive, relying on Tony to carry the mission forward while he enters a trance under duress.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His inability to maintain clarity or agency leaves the mission vulnerable to manipulation and moral compromise.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Final Gambit
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Driven by the goal to win five souls and save Valentina, Parnassus moves from reluctant acceptance of the wager to near-success, only to face betrayal and ultimately intervene to preserve the contest's integrity.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: He accepts the new 'first to five' wager with Mr. Nick, reigniting his purpose.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He thwarts Tony's attempt to cheat the system, ensuring the soul contest remains valid, though Valentina's fate remains ambiguous.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Mr. Nick refuses to confirm whether Valentina is truly free, leaving the ultimate success of Parnassus's goal uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Truth, Deception, and Redemption
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Parnassus navigates a web of deception—from Tony's hidden identity to Mr. Nick's manipulative offers—culminating in a final act of sacrificial truth to break illusion and reclaim moral authority.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: He is thrown into crisis when the Man from Mr. Nick loses his memory, disrupting the expected spiritual confrontation.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He actively intervenes to stop Tony's deception, using magic and sacrifice to uphold the integrity of the soul contest.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The cost of truth—Tony's death and Valentina's ambiguous freedom—leaves moral and emotional consequences unresolved.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Reclamation of Identity
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Tony evolves from a man with no memory of his past to one who gradually uncovers and embraces his identity as Anthony Shepherd, a figure tied to charity and moral purpose, ultimately using this reclaimed self to drive transformation within the troupe and the Imaginarium mission.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Tony is amnesiac and desperate to recover his identity and understand why he was hanged.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Tony integrates his past into a new sense of self, using it to advocate for change and lead the troupe forward.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The full truth behind his hanging and the consequences of his past actions remain obscured.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Rivalry and Recognition: Tony vs. Anton
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Tony's rising influence within the troupe intensifies his rivalry with Anton, shifting from subtle competition to open confrontation, culminating in public exposure and psychological unraveling as Anton's accusations threaten Tony's legitimacy and control.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Anton resents Tony's favor with Parnassus and the implied reward of Valentina's hand.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Tony's public persona collapses under Anton's revelations, exposing deep fractures in his identity and authority.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Anton's appearances are real or hallucinatory remains ambiguous, leaving the nature of their conflict unresolved.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Outsider to Successor
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Tony ascends from a mysterious amnesiac to a central figure in the troupe's mission, eventually positioning himself as Parnassus's successor and Valentina's chosen partner, achieving elite social status before his dramatic fall from grace.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Tony is an unknown figure, conditionally accepted into the group based on potential.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Tony reaches the pinnacle of public and romantic validation, only to have his status violently stripped away by the exposure of his past.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The legitimacy of Tony's rise—whether earned or illusory—and the possibility of redemption remain open.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Moral Transformation and Self-Deception
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Tony moves from denial and justification of his criminal past to moments of genuine remorse and moral testing, yet ultimately regresses into violence and self-destruction, revealing the fragility of his redemption and the persistence of inner corruption.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Tony admits fault but frames his actions as ethically necessary within a corrupt system.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Tony's moral facade collapses as he commits a violent act against a child, exposing his inability to transcend his darker impulses.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Tony was ever truly capable of change or was always destined to fail the moral trials of the Imaginarium remains ambiguous.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Charmed Life and Cosmic Significance
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Tony is gradually revealed to be more than a mere mortal participant in Parnassus's wager—he is a figure of supernatural importance, protected by mysterious markings and central to Mr. Nick's repeated failures, suggesting a hidden destiny that transcends his own understanding.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Tony overhears cryptic warnings about the 'First to Five' game, unaware of his role in it.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Tony dies believing he outwitted fate, but his body and the brass tube become instruments in a larger cosmic game, underscoring his enigmatic significance.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The origin and power of Tony's markings, and whether he might return or influence future outcomes, remain unexplained.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Burden of Truth: Valentina's Quest for Answers
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Valentina transitions from passive curiosity to active confrontation in her pursuit of the truth about her father's past and her own fate. Initially frustrated by Parnassus's evasiveness, she becomes the emotional catalyst for revelation, culminating in her shattering discovery that she is the stakes of a wager with Mr. Nick.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Valentina seeks closure about her father's cryptic story and feels emotionally isolated by his silence.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She learns the devastating truth that her soul is bound to Mr. Nick on her sixteenth birthday, leading to betrayal and emotional rupture.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Her ability to reconcile with her father and participate in the contest to save her soul remains uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Object to Agent: Valentina's Assertion of Autonomy
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Valentina evolves from being treated as a symbolic prize in a male-driven contest to becoming the decisive authority in moments of crisis. She shifts from passive objectification to active leadership, especially in caregiving and moral decision-making, redefining her status within the troupe.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Her romantic future is proposed as a reward in a soul-winning competition without her consent.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She assumes sole responsibility for her father's care and removes others from positions of influence, asserting full emotional and practical control.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Despite her growing authority, structural power imbalances and external supernatural forces continue to threaten her autonomy.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Love, Rejection, and Emotional Crossroads
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Valentina navigates conflicting emotional attachments—to Anton, to George/Tony, and to the troupe—revealing her internal struggle between desire for escape and loyalty. Her relationships become a mirror for her identity crisis, marked by rejection, flirtation, and suppressed longing.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: She rejects Anton's earnest proposal with irritation, masking her own yearning for freedom and connection.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She momentarily re-engages with the mission after emotional appeals from Tony, showing her vulnerability to romantic and familial bonds despite her desire to flee.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Her true romantic allegiance and emotional availability remain ambiguous, leaving her relationships in limbo.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Moral Courage and Defiance in Crisis
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Valentina emerges as a moral leader during the hanged man crisis, repeatedly defying authority and group consensus to act with compassion and decisiveness. Her actions create tension but also establish her as the ethical center of the troupe.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: She takes charge to verify and respond to a life-threatening situation, shifting from observer to leader.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She revives the man and hides him against orders, directly challenging Parnassus and Anton to protect a stranger.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Her defiance strains group cohesion and foreshadows deeper conflict, especially with Anton's jealousy and Parnassus's authority.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Reclaiming Identity: Valentina's Journey to Self-Affirmation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Valentina moves from emotional exhaustion and desire for escape to a reclaimed sense of self, culminating in her confident assertion of independence in her suburban life. Her arc bridges the fantastical and the real, showing her transformation from a trapped daughter to a self-determined woman.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: She expresses a desire to surrender to the police, emotionally drained and disillusioned with the Imaginarium.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: She confidently presents her family life to her returned father, asserting her contentment and autonomy without seeking validation.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Her father's unresolved guilt and the supernatural past may resurface, threatening the stability of her hard-won identity.
